narration: That 'four easy payments' button is literally hijacking your wallet. BNPL users spend forty-five percent more per purchase — and most have no idea.
visual: Smartphone showing BNPL checkout screen with '4 EASY PAYMENTS OF $25' button glowing red. Large '45%' stat flies in from the right side.

narration: Here's why — your brain doesn't process four twenty-five dollar payments as one hundred dollars. It files each one as 'small,' and that mental trick destroys your budget.
visual: Brain diagram split down the middle: left side shows a large red pulsing $100 circle (cash pain signal active), right side shows four tiny gray $25 circles (BNPL barely registers).

narration: Add up your active BNPL loans across all your apps right now. The average person has three to four running at the same time — totaling over nine hundred dollars.
visual: Three BNPL loan cards (Klarna, Afterpay, Affirm) slide in one by one with their balances. A running counter climbs from $0 to $940 at the bottom.

narration: And thirty-four percent of users miss at least one payment. Every late hit costs up to thirty-four dollars — and those fees stack up faster than you'd think.
visual: April calendar with four payment due dates highlighted in red. X marks appear on each missed date one by one. A fee counter below climbs: $34, $68, $102, $136.

narration: Factor in those late fees and some BNPL plans hit the equivalent of twenty-five percent APR — the same as a high-interest credit card, just dressed up as 'free financing.'
visual: Side-by-side bar chart: credit card bar grows to ~20% APR, BNPL bar grows taller to ~25% APR. 'FREE FINANCING' text is crossed out below.

narration: The rule that actually works: one purchase, one payment. If you can't write down your total BNPL balance from memory right now, that's your wake-up call. Start today.
visual: Piggy bank with coins falling in from above. Bold CTA banner slides up: 'ONE PURCHASE ONE PAYMENT'. Sub-text: 'OPEN YOUR APPS. ADD IT UP.'
